zotero.abstractNote: Few-shot segmentation aims to segment objects of unseen classes given only a few annotated examples. While existing methods achieve impressive results, they typically assume that all classes are available at training time or that the model is trained on a fixed set of base classes. In this paper, we address the more challenging and realistic scenario of Incremental Few-Shot Segmentation (IFSS), where the model is required to learn new classes sequentially without forgetting the previously learned ones. We propose a novel prototype-based approach that relies on a shared embedding space and a memory-based mechanism to store and update class prototypes. Our method effectively mitigates catastrophic forgetting by leveraging a distillation loss and a prototype-based replay strategy. We evaluate our approach on the PASCAL-5i and COCO-20i datasets, demonstrating that it significantly outperforms existing baselines in the incremental setting.
